title: Fixing Invalid Git Tag Name Validation

statement.markdown: Write a function to validate Git tag names. A valid tag name consists of uppercase and lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens. It cannot start or end with a hyphen, and it cannot contain consecutive hyphens. For example, 'v1.2.3' is valid, but 'v1-2-3', 'v-1.2.3', and 'v1--2-3' are not valid. You will receive a string as input representing a potential Git tag name and must return `true` if it's valid and `false` otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing a potential Git tag name.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the tag name is valid.
statement.notes: You can assume the input string only contains valid ASCII characters.

examples.explanation: Tag name cannot contain consecutive hyphens
Tag name cannot start with a hyphen
Tag name cannot end with a hyphen

hints.text: First hint text: Check for consecutive hyphens.
Second hint text: Check for leading/trailing hyphens.

editorial.title: Editorial title: Validating Git Tag Names
editorial.intuition: Explanation of the correct approach: Iterate through the string and check for valid characters and hyphen rules.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach summary: Use a brute force approach with multiple if-else statements to check for the rules.
editorial.betterSolution: Better solution: Use a regular expression to match the valid tag name pattern.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: Use a regular expression to match the valid tag name pattern and check for consecutive hyphens and leading/trailing hyphens.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof: Iterate through the string, check for valid characters, and ensure the string meets the hyphen rules.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: 'v1.2.3' returns true, 'v1-2-3' returns false because of consecutive hyphens.
commonMistakes: Mistake 1: Not checking for consecutive hyphens.
Mistake 2: Not checking for leading/trailing hyphens.
